## [00:00 - 01:47]

What's five plus five? Ten. What's eight plus two? Ten. Okay. What's nine plus one? Ten. Okay. What's twenty divided by two? Ten. Okay. These are all the different ways in which we can get to ten. There is an infinite number of ways to get to ten. What is ten? It represents happiness. It represents financial freedom. It represents good health. It represents fulfillment. The problem I think that we have currently in society is there is a bunch of people who say their way to get to ten is the only way to get to ten. It's wrong. I don't believe that for a moment now. I think the joy in life, the beauty in life is you define your own path towards ten and be really wary when someone says this is the only way to get to ten. Because you ask ten different people that and they'll show you ten different ways like you experienced. And it's not even about asking ten people and trying to find the through line. The thing is to try to find the you line. What's your line to ten? I think there is benefit though in copying other people's recipes. Oh, definitely. I only take the advice of people, I feel, that have a level of credibility to it. Either that they've taught others that have then achieved the outcome that you want or they've done it themselves and they've achieved that outcome. And maybe multiple times. If you're copying the recipe of someone that's never cooked a dish, your odds of that giving you great outcome probably won't be that high. So then I'm gonna reshape what I said before. Find a group of people who are credible, have walked the talk and talked the walk and done both. Look at how they've gotten to ten. But then while you've got all the different formulas of how to get to ten, don't be afraid to add something yourself to that formula too. I think be courageous in having a part of that formula that you've put in.
